title: Extract Tables from Annual Reports for Financial Analysis
metaTitle: Extract Tables from Annual Reports | Financial Analysis Tool
metaDescription: Convert financial tables from annual reports to Excel for analysis. AI-powered extraction from PDF reports with 99%+ accuracy.
h1: Extract Tables from Annual Reports for Financial Analysis

hero.headline: Convert Financial Tables from Annual Reports to Excel
hero.subheadline: Extract income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow tables from PDF annual reports into structured spreadsheets for investment analysis

summary: Financial analysts need to extract tables from annual reports to perform comparative analysis, build models, and generate investment insights. GridPull converts financial tables from PDF annual reports into structured Excel files, eliminating manual data entry and reducing errors in your analysis workflow.
whoItsFor: Investment analysts comparing financial metrics across companies
Research teams analyzing quarterly and annual earnings data
whenThisIsRelevant: Analyzing multiple companies in the same sector for comparison
Building discounted cash flow models from historical financial data
Creating investment presentations with standardized financial metrics
supportedInputs: PDF annual reports from SEC filings
PNG or JPEG images of financial statements
expectedOutputs: Excel files with separate sheets for each financial statement
CSV files containing structured financial data with proper column headers
commonChallenges: Manual retyping introduces calculation errors in financial models
Complex multi-column financial tables are time-intensive to recreate
Inconsistent formatting across different company reports slows analysis
Legacy scanned reports require OCR to access underlying data
howItWorksSteps: Upload PDF annual reports or scanned financial documents
AI identifies and extracts table structures including headers and numerical data
Review extracted fields and customize any specific financial metrics needed
Export to Excel or CSV with proper formatting for immediate analysis
whyPdfExcelAiFits: Handles complex multi-column financial tables common in annual reports
OCR capability processes both digital PDFs and scanned legacy documents
Batch processing allows analysis of multiple companies simultaneously
99%+ accuracy on clear financial documents reduces manual verification time
limitations: Very complex nested tables with multiple sub-categories may require manual review
Heavily redacted or low-quality scanned reports may have missing data fields
Non-standard financial statement layouts may need custom field configuration

faq.question: Can this handle 10-K filings from the SEC database?
faq.answer: Yes, the tool processes digital PDF 10-K filings and can extract standard financial statement tables including income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements.

faq.question: How accurate is the extraction for financial numbers?
faq.answer: The tool achieves 99%+ accuracy on clear financial documents, though complex nested tables or poor scan quality may require manual verification of extracted numbers.

faq.question: Can I process multiple annual reports at once?
faq.answer: Yes, batch processing allows you to upload multiple annual reports simultaneously and extract financial tables from all of them in a single workflow.

faq.question: What if the annual report has non-standard financial statement formatting?
faq.answer: The AI adapts to most layouts, but non-standard formats may require custom field selection to properly identify and extract the specific financial metrics you need.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing
Supports both digital and scanned annual reports
Free trial available to test with your specific reports
exampleUseCases: Extract income statements from 5 competing companies for sector analysis
Convert quarterly cash flow data into Excel for DCF model building
Process historical annual reports to build 10-year financial trend analysis
Extract balance sheet data from scanned legacy reports for historical research

reasons: Primary keyword not in meta description
